---
title: "About"
page-layout: full
---

<div class="hero">

<p>
This website is being built as a collection of interactive teaching tools for computational mathematics.
The goal is to make numerical methods feel more concrete by letting students experiment with the algorithms directly.
</p>

</div>

<div class="explanation">

## Purpose

Many numerical methods are introduced through formulas and tables of iterates. Those are important, but they can sometimes hide the main idea of the method.

The apps on this site are meant to give students a more visual way to explore the algorithms. For example, in root finding, students can choose an initial point, press a button, and see exactly how the next approximation is produced.

## Current focus

The current module is root finding. It includes:

- Fixed point iteration
- Newton's method

More topics can be added later as the course materials develop.

</div>
